entries.name: Entity definitions list
entries.description: Documented, growing list of entity types relevant to heritage science (objects, people, organisations, samples, methods, equipment, results, documents, etc.) with descriptions and scope notes.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Provides the conceptual basis for any schema work above T02.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: HPSWG-Models, HSDS, E-RIHS DIGILAB, aggregator communities.
entries.notes: Foundational artefact, prerequisite for most other work.

entries.name: Standardised metadata schemas
entries.description: Documented field definitions for each entity, with required and optional fields, priority indicators, and impact notes.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Defines what T03 and T04b mean in practice for each entity.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Partial, fragmented across communities (Spectrum, LIDO, project-specific schemas).
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: Collections Trust, aggregator communities, project-specific groups.
entries.notes: The bridge between entity definitions and practical recording.

entries.name: Reference vocabularies
entries.description: Published, reusable controlled vocabularies for the values used in metadata schemas.
entries.handlesResourcesNotes: R13, plus values within most other resources.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables T05 by providing the vocabularies to align against.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Strong for some domains (AAT, Wikidata), gaps in heritage science specifics.

entries.name: Recommended semantic templates
entries.description: Documented, community-endorsed modelling patterns showing how entities and relationships are expressed in CIDOC CRM and its extensions.
entries.handlesResourcesNotes: R03 through R16 once at T04b.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables T07b by providing the patterns, supports automated lifts from T04b/T06+.
entries.currentStatusNotes: In active development through HPSWG-Models, alignment with CRM-SIG pending.
entries.notes: Core to the templates/recommended implementations work.

entries.name: Schema-to-schema mapping tools
entries.description: Automated or semi-automated tools to map between different shared schemas (Spectrum to LIDO, institutional schemas to aggregator schemas, etc.).
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Lowers the barrier to schema alignment.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: Aggregators (ArtUK, Museum Data Service, Europeana), institutional IT.

entries.name: Schema-to-vocabulary mapping tools
entries.description: Tools that suggest or apply mappings from local terminology to published vocabularies.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables T05 without requiring manual remapping of legacy data.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Some exist for specific vocabulary pairs, fragmented.
entries.notes: Often a major barrier to T05 adoption.

entries.name: PID minting and registration services
entries.description: Services that issue and register persistent identifiers for resources and entities.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables T06 by providing the identifier infrastructure.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Strong for some PID types (DOI, ORCID, ROR), emerging for others (IGSN for samples).
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: DataCite, Crossref, ORCID, IGSN/SESAR, ROR, Handle, ARK communities.
entries.notes: Coverage varies by PID type and resource.

entries.name: Automatic metadata-to-semantic mapping tools
entries.description: Tools that take metadata at T04b or T06+ and produce semantic representation at T07b using recommended templates.
entries.handlesResourcesNotes: R03 through R16 once at T04b.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables the jump from rich metadata to semantic representation without manual modelling.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: HPSWG-Models tooling, EasyRDF-based converters, research projects.
entries.notes: Critical for making T07b achievable at scale.

entries.name: Knowledge graph platforms (ResearchSpace, Arches, others)
entries.description: Platforms that host, edit, and serve semantic representations.
entries.handlesResourcesNotes: R03 through R16 at T07b and T08b.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Provide the working environment for semantically represented data.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Mature (ResearchSpace, Arches), well-adopted in heritage and conservation.
entries.notes: Integration with mapping tools is the practical bridge.

entries.name: Linked data publication infrastructure
entries.description: SPARQL endpoints, linked data fragments, IIIF endpoints, federation registries.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables federation by providing the publication layer.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Mature in some communities, fragmented in heritage science.
entries.notes: Coordination across infrastructures is the gap.

entries.name: Specific documented Digital Twin models
entries.description: Documented specifications for what constitutes a Digital Twin in a given context, including required components, structure, and use cases.
entries.handlesResourcesNotes: R03 through R16 at T08b plus integration spec.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Defines what T09 means in practice for a given domain or use case.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: E-RIHS, ECHOES, research communities, domain experts.
entries.notes: Specification work needed before tooling is meaningful.

entries.name: Digital Twin construction and mapping tools
entries.description: Tools that assemble resources at T08b into a documented Digital Twin construct according to a specification.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Enables T09 by providing the assembly infrastructure.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: Future work, likely under ECCCH or similar.

entries.name: Data management platform integration
entries.description: Connectors and plugins that integrate the framework's schemas, vocabularies, and templates into general data management tools (research data management systems, electronic lab notebooks, asset management).
entries.supportsLevelsNotes: T02 to T04b primarily, possibly T05 and T06.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Makes data management happen as part of work rather than after the fact.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: Institutional research data services, ELN providers, asset management vendors.
entries.notes: The 'happens as you work' principle depends on this.

entries.name: General data management procedures and guidance
entries.description: Documented procedures, training materials, and best practice guidance for researchers and institutions.
entries.supportsLevelsNotes: T01 to T04b primarily, with references upward.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Helps researchers locate themselves and plan progression.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: Institutional research data offices, training networks, RICHeS HSDS.
entries.notes: The human side of the framework.

entries.name: Self-assessment and planning tools
entries.description: Tools that let a researcher or institution assess their current position in the framework and plan progression.
entries.ownersAndInitiatives: Future work, likely a deliverable of this initiative itself.
entries.notes: The framework needs this to be usable in practice.

entries.name: Federation and aggregation services
entries.description: Services that aggregate metadata and semantic representations across institutions.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: Provide the federation layer where it does not already exist.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Strong in some sectors (ArtUK, Europeana, Museum Data Service), gaps in heritage science specifics.
entries.notes: Existing infrastructure to build on, not duplicate.

entries.name: Recommended implementation registry
entries.description: A federated registry holding recommended semantic templates, schemas, vocabularies, mappings, and application profiles, with versioning and governance.
entries.handlesResourcesNotes: Holds reference resources S01, S02, S03, S04, S06, S11 rather than resources directly.
entries.enablesTransitionsNotes: The registry concept from the E-RIHS DIGILAB response, supports all higher-level work.
entries.currentStatusNotes: Conceptual, scoping under way through E-RIHS DIGILAB.
